---
title: "GBM Downstream Analysis: Primary vs Recurrent TME"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-fold: true
    embed-resources: true
    theme: flatly
jupyter: python3
---

# Setup

```{python}
import scanpy as sc
import decoupler as dc
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.ticker as mticker
import seaborn as sns
import warnings
import os

warnings.filterwarnings("ignore")
pd.options.future.infer_string = False

sc.settings.verbosity = 0
sc.set_figure_params(dpi=100, facecolor="white", frameon=False)
```

# Load Annotated Data

```{python}
adata = sc.read_h5ad("h5ad_files/annotated.h5ad")

# Sanity check
print(adata)
print("\nCell types:", adata.obs["cell_type"].unique().tolist())
print("Conditions:", adata.obs["condition"].unique().tolist())
print("Samples:", adata.obs["sample"].unique().tolist())
print("\nCells per sample:")
print(adata.obs.groupby(["sample", "condition"]).size())
```

# Cell Type Composition

## Overall proportions per sample

```{python}
# Compute proportions within each sample
comp = (
    adata.obs
    .groupby(["sample", "condition", "cell_type"], observed=True)
    .size()
    .reset_index(name="n")
)
comp["proportion"] = comp.groupby("sample")["n"].transform(lambda x: x / x.sum())

# Consistent cell type colour palette
cell_types_ordered = [
    "Tumor", "Microglia", "TAM_APOE", "TAM_SPP1", "TAM_macrophage",
    "Monocyte", "CD8_T_cell", "OPC", "Oligodendrocyte", "Endothelial"
]
palette = dict(zip(cell_types_ordered, sns.color_palette("tab10", len(cell_types_ordered))))

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 5))
pivot = comp.pivot_table(index="sample", columns="cell_type", values="proportion", fill_value=0)
pivot = pivot.reindex(columns=cell_types_ordered, fill_value=0)

# Sort samples: primaries first, recurrent last
sample_order = sorted(pivot.index, key=lambda x: (0 if "primary" in x else 1, x))
pivot = pivot.loc[sample_order]

pivot.plot(kind="bar", stacked=True, ax=ax, color=[palette.get(c, "grey") for c in pivot.columns], width=0.7)
ax.set_ylabel("Proportion of cells")
ax.set_xlabel("")
ax.set_xticklabels(ax.get_xticklabels(), rotation=30, ha="right")
ax.legend(bbox_to_anchor=(1.01, 1), loc="upper left", fontsize=9, title="Cell type")

# Mark condition boundary
n_primary = sum("primary" in s for s in sample_order)
ax.axvline(n_primary - 0.5, color="black", linestyle="--", linewidth=1.2, label="recurrence →")
ax.set_title("Cell type composition per sample")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

## Primary vs Recurrent — mean proportions

```{python}
mean_comp = (
    comp.groupby(["condition", "cell_type"], observed=True)["proportion"]
    .mean()
    .reset_index()
)

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(12, 5), sharey=True)
for ax, cond in zip(axes, ["primary", "recurrent"]):
    sub = (mean_comp[mean_comp["condition"] == cond]
           .set_index("cell_type")[["proportion"]]
           .reindex(cell_types_ordered, fill_value=0))
    bars = ax.barh(sub.index, sub["proportion"],
                   color=[palette.get(c, "grey") for c in sub.index])
    ax.set_xlim(0, 0.75)
    ax.set_title(f"{cond.capitalize()} (n={'3' if cond=='primary' else '1'})")
    ax.set_xlabel("Mean proportion")
    ax.xaxis.set_major_formatter(mticker.PercentFormatter(xmax=1))
    ax.invert_yaxis()

plt.suptitle("Mean cell type proportions by condition", y=1.02)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

## Raw cell counts per condition

```{python}
counts_tbl = (
    adata.obs
    .groupby(["condition", "cell_type"], observed=True)
    .size()
    .unstack(fill_value=0)
)
counts_tbl = counts_tbl.reindex(columns=cell_types_ordered, fill_value=0)

display(counts_tbl.style
        .background_gradient(axis=1, cmap="Blues")
        .format("{:,}"))
```

# TAM Subtype Analysis

> TAMs were sub-clustered into four states: **TAM_APOE** (immunosuppressive), **TAM_SPP1**
> (pro-tumorigenic), **TAM_macrophage** (intermediate), and **Monocyte** (infiltrating).
> Neither Paper 1 nor Paper 2 resolved TAMs at this resolution.

## TAM proportion shifts primary → recurrent

```{python}
tam_types = ["TAM_APOE", "TAM_SPP1", "TAM_macrophage", "Monocyte"]

tam_comp = (
    adata.obs[adata.obs["cell_type"].isin(tam_types)]
    .groupby(["sample", "condition", "cell_type"], observed=True)
    .size()
    .reset_index(name="n")
)
tam_comp["tam_proportion"] = (
    tam_comp.groupby("sample")["n"].transform(lambda x: x / x.sum())
)

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 4))
tam_palette = dict(zip(tam_types, sns.color_palette("Set2", 4)))
pivot_tam = tam_comp.pivot_table(
    index="sample", columns="cell_type", values="tam_proportion", fill_value=0
)
pivot_tam = pivot_tam.reindex(columns=tam_types, fill_value=0)
pivot_tam = pivot_tam.loc[sample_order]

pivot_tam.plot(kind="bar", stacked=True, ax=ax,
               color=[tam_palette[c] for c in pivot_tam.columns], width=0.7)
ax.axvline(n_primary - 0.5, color="black", linestyle="--", linewidth=1.2)
ax.set_ylabel("Proportion within TAMs")
ax.set_xlabel("")
ax.set_xticklabels(ax.get_xticklabels(), rotation=30, ha="right")
ax.legend(bbox_to_anchor=(1.01, 1), loc="upper left", fontsize=9, title="TAM subtype")
ax.set_title("TAM subtype composition per sample")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

## TAM subtype marker genes

```{python}
tam_markers = {
    "TAM_APOE":       ["APOE", "CD163", "SPP1", "C1QA", "C1QB"],
    "TAM_SPP1":       ["SPP1", "CD14", "CCL2", "VEGFA", "MMP9"],
    "TAM_macrophage": ["CD68", "CD163", "MRC1", "CSF1R", "ITGAM"],
    "Monocyte":       ["CD14", "FCGR3A", "CX3CR1", "S100A8", "S100A9"],
}

tams = adata[adata.obs["cell_type"].isin(tam_types)].copy()
sc.pl.dotplot(
    tams, tam_markers, groupby="cell_type",
    standard_scale="var", swap_axes=False,
    title="TAM subtype marker expression"
)
```

# UMAP — Condition and Cell Type

## Primary vs Recurrent split

```{python}
cond_palette = {"primary": "#4C72B0", "recurrent": "#DD8452"}

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(14, 6))

# Primaries greyed out, recurrent highlighted
sc.pl.umap(adata, color="condition", palette=cond_palette,
           groups=["recurrent"], na_color="#DDDDDD",
           ax=axes[0], show=False,
           title="Recurrent cells highlighted")

# Full condition split with equal weight
sc.pl.umap(adata, color="condition", palette=cond_palette,
           ax=axes[1], show=False,
           title="Condition (primary vs recurrent)")

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

## Condition split per cell type

```{python}
# One panel per cell type — shows where recurrent cells sit within each cluster
fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 5, figsize=(20, 8))
axes = axes.flatten()

for ax, ct in zip(axes, cell_types_ordered):
    sub = adata[adata.obs["cell_type"] == ct].copy()
    sc.pl.umap(adata, ax=ax, show=False, color="cell_type",
               groups=[ct], na_color="#EEEEEE", legend_loc=None)
    # Overlay condition colours for this cell type only
    for cond, col in cond_palette.items():
        mask = sub.obs["condition"] == cond
        coords = sub[mask.values].obsm["X_umap"]
        ax.scatter(coords[:, 0], coords[:, 1], s=2, alpha=0.6,
                   color=col, label=cond, rasterized=True)
    ax.set_title(ct, fontsize=9)
    ax.set_xlabel(""); ax.set_ylabel("")

# shared legend on last panel
axes[-1].legend(*axes[0].get_legend_handles_labels(),
                loc="center", fontsize=9, title="Condition",
                markerscale=4)

plt.suptitle("Primary (blue) vs Recurrent (orange) within each cell type", y=1.01)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

## Integration check — sample separation

> **Note:** In the sample-coloured UMAP, `primary_001` appears largely isolated in
> its own region, suggesting scVI did not fully integrate it with the other primary
> samples. Check whether this reflects biology (a biologically distinct tumour) or
> a residual batch effect before interpreting primary vs recurrent differences.

```{python}
sample_palette = {
    "primary_001":  "#1f77b4",
    "primary_002":  "#2ca02c",
    "primary_003":  "#ff7f0e",
    "recurrent_001":"#d62728",
}

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(14, 6))

sc.pl.umap(adata, color="sample", palette=sample_palette,
           ax=axes[0], show=False, title="All samples")

# primary_001 vs rest
adata.obs["primary_001_flag"] = adata.obs["sample"].apply(
    lambda x: "primary_001" if x == "primary_001" else "other"
)
sc.pl.umap(adata, color="primary_001_flag",
           palette={"primary_001": "#1f77b4", "other": "#DDDDDD"},
           ax=axes[1], show=False,
           title="primary_001 isolated (possible batch outlier?)")

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

```{python}
# QC check: are primary_001 cells different quality?
qc_cols = ["n_genes_by_counts", "total_counts", "pct_counts_mt"]
available_qc = [c for c in qc_cols if c in adata.obs.columns]

if available_qc:
    sc.pl.violin(adata, available_qc, groupby="sample",
                 multi_panel=True, jitter=0.2, stripplot=False,
                 rotation=30)
else:
    print("QC metrics not stored in obs — re-run sc.pp.calculate_qc_metrics if needed.")
```

# Differential Expression: Primary vs Recurrent

> **Statistical note:** With n=3 primary and n=1 recurrent sample, pseudobulk DE
> is underpowered — variance cannot be estimated from a single replicate.
> Below we use a **per-cell Wilcoxon rank-sum test** (all cells pooled within condition)
> which provides gene rankings but inflates significance due to pseudoreplication.
> Treat results as hypothesis-generating, not confirmatory.

## Tumor cells

```{python}
tumor = adata[adata.obs["cell_type"] == "Tumor"].copy()

sc.tl.rank_genes_groups(
    tumor,
    groupby="condition",
    groups=["recurrent"],
    reference="primary",
    method="wilcoxon",
    layer="counts",
    use_raw=False,
    key_added="de_tumor"
)

de_tumor = sc.get.rank_genes_groups_df(tumor, group="recurrent", key="de_tumor")
de_tumor = de_tumor.rename(columns={"names": "gene"})

print(f"Upregulated in recurrent Tumor (logFC > 0.5, p_adj < 0.05): "
      f"{((de_tumor.logfoldchanges > 0.5) & (de_tumor.pvals_adj < 0.05)).sum()}")
print(f"Downregulated in recurrent Tumor (logFC < -0.5, p_adj < 0.05): "
      f"{((de_tumor.logfoldchanges < -0.5) & (de_tumor.pvals_adj < 0.05)).sum()}")

display(de_tumor.head(20))
```

### Volcano plot — Tumor cells

```{python}
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 6))

de = de_tumor.copy()
de["-log10_padj"] = -np.log10(de["pvals_adj"].clip(lower=1e-300))
de["sig"] = (de["pvals_adj"] < 0.05) & (de["logfoldchanges"].abs() > 0.5)

ax.scatter(de.loc[~de["sig"], "logfoldchanges"],
           de.loc[~de["sig"], "-log10_padj"],
           alpha=0.3, s=8, color="lightgrey")
up   = de[de["sig"] & (de["logfoldchanges"] > 0)]
down = de[de["sig"] & (de["logfoldchanges"] < 0)]
ax.scatter(up["logfoldchanges"],   up["-log10_padj"],   alpha=0.7, s=12, color="#DD8452", label=f"Up in recurrent ({len(up)})")
ax.scatter(down["logfoldchanges"], down["-log10_padj"], alpha=0.7, s=12, color="#4C72B0", label=f"Up in primary ({len(down)})")

# Label top 10 each direction
for _, row in pd.concat([up.nlargest(10, "-log10_padj"), down.nlargest(10, "-log10_padj")]).iterrows():
    ax.text(row["logfoldchanges"], row["-log10_padj"], row["gene"],
            fontsize=7, ha="left", va="bottom")

ax.axvline(0.5,  color="black", linestyle="--", linewidth=0.8)
ax.axvline(-0.5, color="black", linestyle="--", linewidth=0.8)
ax.axhline(-np.log10(0.05), color="black", linestyle=":", linewidth=0.8)
ax.set_xlabel("log2 fold change (recurrent vs primary)")
ax.set_ylabel("-log10 adjusted p-value")
ax.set_title("Tumor cells: recurrent vs primary")
ax.legend(fontsize=9)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

### Top recurrence-upregulated genes in Tumor cells

```{python}
top_up_tumor = de_tumor[de_tumor["logfoldchanges"] > 0].nsmallest(6, "pvals_adj")["gene"].tolist()

sc.pl.violin(
    tumor, top_up_tumor,
    groupby="condition",
    use_raw=False, layer="counts",
    palette={"primary": "#4C72B0", "recurrent": "#DD8452"},
    multi_panel=True,
    jitter=0.2, stripplot=True
)
```

## TAM cells (all subtypes pooled)

```{python}
tam_all = adata[adata.obs["cell_type"].isin(tam_types)].copy()

sc.tl.rank_genes_groups(
    tam_all,
    groupby="condition",
    groups=["recurrent"],
    reference="primary",
    method="wilcoxon",
    layer="counts",
    use_raw=False,
    key_added="de_tam"
)

de_tam = sc.get.rank_genes_groups_df(tam_all, group="recurrent", key="de_tam")
de_tam = de_tam.rename(columns={"names": "gene"})

print(f"Upregulated in recurrent TAMs (logFC > 0.5, p_adj < 0.05): "
      f"{((de_tam.logfoldchanges > 0.5) & (de_tam.pvals_adj < 0.05)).sum()}")

display(de_tam.head(20))
```

### Top recurrence-upregulated genes in TAMs

```{python}
top_up_tam = de_tam[de_tam["logfoldchanges"] > 0].nsmallest(6, "pvals_adj")["gene"].tolist()

sc.pl.violin(
    tam_all, top_up_tam,
    groupby="condition",
    use_raw=False, layer="counts",
    palette={"primary": "#4C72B0", "recurrent": "#DD8452"},
    multi_panel=True,
    jitter=0.2, stripplot=True
)
```

# Pseudobulk Aggregation

Aggregate raw counts per sample × cell type. This is the input you would use for
a proper pseudobulk DE tool (e.g. DESeq2) once more recurrent replicates are available.

```{python}
psbulk = dc.pp.pseudobulk(
    adata,
    sample_col="sample",
    groups_col="cell_type",
    layer="counts",
    mode="sum"
)

print("Pseudobulk object:")
print(psbulk)
print("\nCells per pseudobulk sample:")
print(psbulk.obs[["psbulk_cells", "psbulk_counts"]].to_string())
```

## QC: cells per pseudobulk sample

```{python}
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 4))
psbulk.obs["label"] = psbulk.obs.index.astype(str)
psbulk.obs["condition"] = psbulk.obs["label"].apply(
    lambda x: "recurrent" if "recurrent" in x else "primary"
)

sns.barplot(
    data=psbulk.obs.sort_values("psbulk_cells", ascending=False),
    x="label", y="psbulk_cells",
    hue="condition",
    palette={"primary": "#4C72B0", "recurrent": "#DD8452"},
    ax=ax
)
ax.set_xlabel("")
ax.set_xticklabels(ax.get_xticklabels(), rotation=45, ha="right", fontsize=8)
ax.set_ylabel("Number of cells")
ax.set_title("Cells per pseudobulk sample (sample × cell type)")
ax.get_legend().set_title("Condition")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

# SPP1 Signalling Check

> Paper 2 identified **SPP1→CD44** as the strongest ECM poor-prognosis LR pair.
> Check whether this signal differs between primary and recurrent in our data.

```{python}
genes_of_interest = ["SPP1", "CD44", "FN1", "ITGAV", "ITGB1", "COL4A5", "COL6A1",
                     "LAMA2", "LAMA4", "LAMB1", "LAMC1", "LAMC3"]

# Keep only genes present in the dataset
genes_of_interest = [g for g in genes_of_interest if g in adata.var_names]
print("Genes found:", genes_of_interest)

sc.pl.dotplot(
    adata, genes_of_interest,
    groupby="cell_type",
    standard_scale="var",
    swap_axes=True,
    title="Paper 2 ECM poor-prognosis LR genes across cell types"
)
```

```{python}
# Expression of SPP1 and CD44 per condition and cell type
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(12, 5))
for ax, gene in zip(axes, ["SPP1", "CD44"]):
    if gene not in adata.var_names:
        ax.set_title(f"{gene} not found")
        continue
    expr = pd.DataFrame({
        "expr":      adata[:, gene].layers["counts"].toarray().flatten(),
        "cell_type": adata.obs["cell_type"].values,
        "condition": adata.obs["condition"].values,
    })
    mean_expr = expr.groupby(["cell_type", "condition"])["expr"].mean().reset_index()
    mean_expr = mean_expr[mean_expr["cell_type"].isin(cell_types_ordered)]

    sns.barplot(
        data=mean_expr, x="cell_type", y="expr", hue="condition",
        palette={"primary": "#4C72B0", "recurrent": "#DD8452"},
        ax=ax
    )
    ax.set_xticklabels(ax.get_xticklabels(), rotation=45, ha="right", fontsize=8)
    ax.set_ylabel("Mean raw counts")
    ax.set_title(f"{gene} expression by cell type and condition")
    ax.get_legend().set_title("Condition")

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```
